title: Ask for Help Effectively
summary: Ask for help in a way that makes people want to say yes.
learnerPromise: Ask for help in a way that makes people want to say yes.
whyItMatters: Nobody does everything alone. Knowing how to ask for help saves time, reduces stress, and builds connections.
realLifeUses: Ask a teacher to explain a concept you did not understand.
Request a coworker to show you how to use a tool.
Ask a neighbor to borrow something you need.
Get help from a friend when you are overwhelmed.
youWillLearn: Be specific about what you need.
Choose the right person to ask.
miniChallenge: Ask one person for help with something small today. Be specific.
steps: Figure out exactly what you need help with.
Pick the person most likely able to help you.
Ask directly: 'Can you help me with [specific thing]?'
Explain briefly why you need help if it adds context.
Thank them, and follow up later if needed.
completionCriteria: You ask one specific person for one specific thing.
You get a clear yes or no response.
commonProblems: Being too vague about what you need.
tips: People usually want to help. Asking is a compliment.
Ask when the person is not busy or stressed.
Always say thank you, even if they say no.
